artist.name: TAEMIN
artist.bio: Lee Tae-min, known mononymously as Taemin, is a South Korean singer, dancer, and actor. He debuted as a member of the South Korean boy band Shinee in May 2008 and the supergroup SuperM in 2019, both under SM Entertainment, and has subsequently been labeled by media outlets as the "Idol's Idol" due to the large number of idols citing him as an inspiration. As an actor, Taemin's first role was as Junsu in the 2009 MBC comedy Hilarious Housewives.

bio.extract: Lee Tae-min, known mononymously as Taemin, is a South Korean singer, dancer, and actor. He debuted as a member of the South Korean boy band Shinee in May 2008 and the supergroup SuperM in 2019, both under SM Entertainment, and has subsequently been labeled by media outlets as the "Idol's Idol" due to the large number of idols citing him as an inspiration. As an actor, Taemin's first role was as Junsu in the 2009 MBC comedy Hilarious Housewives.

timeline.title: SHINee debuts
timeline.body: He's SHINee's maknae, and he's 14. Almost nobody had debuted that young before.

timeline.title: ACE — the solo debut
timeline.body: First solo EP. "Danger" took the Gaon album chart and settled whether he could carry something on his own.

timeline.title: PRESS IT
timeline.body: His first full album. Ten tracks, a title song produced by Bruno Mars, and the concept photos people still repost every February. Number one on Gaon.

timeline.title: Sayonara Hitori
timeline.body: Japanese solo debut. The concept film for it was far better than it had any need to be.

timeline.title: MOVE
timeline.body: Second album. The choreography was loose and androgynous in a way male idols mostly weren't doing, and half the industry ended up covering it.

timeline.title: SuperM
timeline.body: SM put him in a supergroup with members of EXO, NCT 127 and WayV. The first album went straight in at number one on the Billboard 200.

timeline.title: Never Gonna Dance Again : Act 1
timeline.body: A two-part album about shedding a version of yourself. This is the setup.

timeline.title: Never Gonna Dance Again : Act 2
timeline.body: The other half. "IDEA" and "Advice" are the performances people still pull up first.

timeline.title: Enlistment
timeline.body: Enlists for his military service, having put out the "Advice" mini-album two weeks earlier. He later moved to public service duty while getting treatment.

timeline.title: Discharge
timeline.body: Out in April 2023. He wrote everyone a letter by hand to say thanks for waiting.

timeline.title: Guilty
timeline.body: The comeback record. Darker, and the staging went all the way into the theatrics.

timeline.title: ETERNAL
timeline.body: The first album he produced himself, out ten years almost to the day after his solo debut.

timeline.title: Veil
timeline.body: First release since moving his solo work over to Galaxy Corporation.

timeline.title: Coachella
timeline.body: First male K-pop soloist to play Coachella. He used the set to premiere six songs nobody had heard yet.

timeline.title: PERMISSION
timeline.body: One of the Coachella songs, finally released properly.

facts: He debuted at 14, which is young even by K-pop standards, and picked up most of his dancing by copying what he saw rather than through formal training.
Other idols call him the Idol's Idol. That one came from inside the industry, not from fans.
Bruno Mars and the Stereotypes produced "Press Your Number".
The MOVE choreography ignored most of the rules about how a male idol was supposed to move. It worked.
ETERNAL in 2024 was the first record he produced himself.
He was the last person cast into SHINee. Scouted on the street, and in the group only a few months before they debuted.
That Press It teaser with two of him almost kissing is ten years old and still gets reposted every February.
SuperM's first album entered the Billboard 200 at number one.
Coachella 2026 made him the first male K-pop soloist the festival had ever booked.

pressIt.title: PRESS IT
pressIt.subtitle: The 1st Album
pressIt.statement: Two of him in the same frame, faces almost touching. Nobody explained it and nobody had to. He was 22, he'd bleached his hair white, and he had very clearly decided he was done being the youngest one in the room. Ten years on it's still the album I go back to first.
